questions.question: Explain the key differences between CPU and GPU architectures in terms of core design and optimization goals.
keyPoints: CPU: Few complex cores optimized for sequential processing
GPU: Many simple cores optimized for parallel processing
CPU: Emphasis on low latency and complex control logic
GPU: Emphasis on high throughput and data parallelism

questions.question: Describe the GPU memory hierarchy and explain when a programmer would choose to use shared memory versus global memory.
keyPoints: Global memory: Large capacity, high latency, accessible to all threads
Shared memory: Small capacity, low latency, accessible only within block
Use shared memory for frequently accessed data that can be reused
Use global memory for large datasets that don't fit in shared memory
Coalesced access patterns important for global memory performance
Bank conflicts consideration for shared memory access
